skills.title: Rig dinghy independently
skills.criteria: Sailor rigs a Tackers dinghy without coach assistance within 10 minutes.

skills.title: Pre-launch safety check
skills.criteria: Sailor verifies PFD fit, bailer, bungs, and tiller attachment before launching.

skills.title: Launch and retrieve dinghy
skills.criteria: Sailor launches from the beach and retrieves the boat safely with minimal assistance.

skills.title: Steer upwind
skills.criteria: Sailor steers a straight course to windward, responding to gusts and lulls with tiller movements.

skills.title: Steer downwind
skills.criteria: Sailor steers downwind, controlling boom position and maintaining fore-aft balance.

skills.title: Tack reliably
skills.criteria: Sailor completes a tack through the wind without stalling or losing significant ground on 3 consecutive attempts.

skills.title: Gybe safely
skills.criteria: Sailor gybes with controlled boom movement, maintaining balance and course through the manoeuvre.

skills.title: Recover from capsize independently
skills.criteria: Sailor rights the boat and climbs back in without coach assistance in flat to small-chop conditions.

skills.title: Self-rescue procedure
skills.criteria: Sailor demonstrates the club standard self-rescue sequence (swim to centreboard, right boat, bail, recommence).

skills.title: Assist another capsized sailor
skills.criteria: Sailor approaches a capsized boat safely from downwind and assists righting without endangering either boat.

skills.title: Identify wind direction
skills.criteria: Sailor identifies wind direction using telltales, ripples, or feel without coach prompting.

skills.title: Sail upwind in 10+ knots
skills.criteria: Sailor maintains boat speed and correct course to windward in moderate breeze, hiking as needed.

skills.title: Understand points of sail
skills.criteria: Sailor can name and demonstrate close-hauled, reaching, and running on request.

skills.title: Consistent tacking technique
skills.criteria: Sailor tacks efficiently across multiple headings in varying wind with no stalls.

skills.title: Gybe in 12+ knots
skills.criteria: Sailor gybes confidently in moderate to fresh breeze, controlling the mainsheet throughout.

skills.title: Sail fast upwind
skills.criteria: Sailor trims sails and hikes to maximise VMG upwind, maintaining course under coach observation.

skills.title: Sail fast downwind
skills.criteria: Sailor controls downwind boat speed and avoids accidental gybes or broaches for 3+ minutes.

skills.title: Windward mark rounding
skills.criteria: Sailor rounds the windward mark on the correct side with a clean tack or bear-away without stalling.

skills.title: Leeward mark rounding
skills.criteria: Sailor rounds the leeward mark with an effective gybe or tack, maintaining speed into the upwind leg.

skills.title: Mark rounding tactics
skills.criteria: Sailor plans the approach, gives room to other boats, and executes an efficient rounding consistently.

skills.title: Understand the start sequence
skills.criteria: Sailor correctly identifies each flag signal and knows the timing of the start countdown.

skills.title: Hit the start line at the gun
skills.criteria: Sailor crosses the start line within 10 seconds of the start signal near a chosen point with coach guidance.

skills.title: Control boat speed in pre-start
skills.criteria: Sailor can slow down and accelerate on demand in the pre-start box without crossing early.

skills.title: Port-starboard right of way
skills.criteria: Sailor understands and applies the port-starboard rule correctly in drill situations.

skills.title: Windward-leeward overlap rights
skills.criteria: Sailor understands which boat must keep clear when boats are overlapped on the same tack.

skills.title: Room at marks
skills.criteria: Sailor correctly claims or gives room at marks, understanding when the zone applies.

skills.title: Identify keelboat parts
skills.criteria: Crew names forestay, shrouds, boom, mainsheet, jib sheet, cleat, winch, and keel correctly.

skills.title: Hoist and lower sails
skills.criteria: Crew hoists mainsail and jib using halyards and lowers them safely under instruction.

skills.title: Understand crew roles
skills.criteria: Crew can describe and take on helm, crew, and foredeck roles and explains the difference.

skills.title: Helm upwind
skills.criteria: Helm steers upwind using telltales and feel to maintain optimal close-hauled angle for 5+ minutes.

skills.title: Crew trim upwind
skills.criteria: Crew trims mainsheet and jib independently and communicates clearly with helm about sail shape.

skills.title: Helm downwind
skills.criteria: Helm steers broad reaching and running without broaching in up to 15 knots.

skills.title: Man overboard recovery
skills.criteria: Crew executes a figure-eight MOB recovery and brings a fender alongside within 3 attempts.

skills.title: Anchor and moorings
skills.criteria: Crew picks up a mooring and deploys anchor safely under instruction.

skills.title: Engine safety and use
skills.criteria: Crew starts engine, uses it to manoeuvre in and out of a berth, and shuts down correctly.
